agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics/courier system with an existing order lifecycle where 'confirmed' has a precise, established meaning (paid + queued for fulfillment + immutable). A new feature request proposes reusing the term 'confirmed' for quotes, but with a fundamentally different semantic meaning (verbal agreement, no payment, not immutable).","bounded_context":"Order Management / Quoting — the collision occurs at the boundary between the Quote lifecycle and the Order lifecycle, both of which share domain vocabulary visible to the same users and developers.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Confirmed Order","definition":"EXISTING: An order that has been paid and is queued for fulfillment. It is immutable — no further changes allowed."},{"term":"Confirmed Quote (proposed)","definition":"PROPOSED: A quote where the customer has verbally agreed to terms but payment has NOT yet occurred. Mutability is unspecified."},{"term":"Quote","definition":"A priced proposal to a customer that precedes an order."},{"term":"Order","definition":"A committed transaction that proceeds through a lifecycle toward fulfillment."},{"term":"Immutable","definition":"Once an entity reaches this state, its core attributes (line items, pricing, addresses) cannot be modified."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"The term 'confirmed' in the Order context means payment has been received and the order is queued for fulfillment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an established domain definition already encoded in the system. Changing its meaning would corrupt existing state transitions, reporting, and downstream integrations.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status 'confirmed', THEN payment has been recorded AND the order is in the fulfillment queue."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A confirmed order is immutable — its line items, pricing, and addresses cannot be changed after confirmation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Immutability after payment is a financial integrity constraint. Allowing mutation post-payment breaks audit trails and fulfillment accuracy.","testable_assertion":"Given a confirmed order, WHEN any mutation is attempted on line items, pricing, or addresses, THEN the system rejects the change."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A quote's 'confirmed' status (as proposed) means verbal agreement only — no payment has occurred.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a proposed business policy for a new workflow step. The semantics could be renamed without system corruption.","testable_assertion":"Given a quote with status 'confirmed', THEN no payment record exists for that quote."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Each term in the ubiquitous language must have exactly one meaning across all bounded contexts that share a user interface or API surface.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Ambiguous domain language causes developer errors, user confusion, incorrect reporting, and integration bugs. This is a foundational DDD principle for system integrity.","testable_assertion":"Given any status term used in the system, WHEN that term appears in any entity, THEN it carries the same semantic meaning (payment state, mutability, lifecycle position)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"The existing order lifecycle uses 'confirmed' to mean paid and immutable","when":"A new status is introduced for quotes representing verbal customer agreement without payment","then":"The new status MUST use a distinct term that does not collide with 'confirmed' (e.g., 'accepted', 'agreed', 'verbally_committed')"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A quote has reached the verbal-agreement status (whatever it is named)","when":"The system displays or references that status","then":"It must be unambiguous to users, developers, and integrators that payment has NOT occurred and the quote is NOT yet an order"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A quote transitions to the verbal-agreement status","when":"Any downstream system or report queries entities with 'confirmed' status","then":"The query MUST NOT return quotes alongside confirmed (paid) orders, as they represent fundamentally different business states"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"The renamed quote status is implemented","when":"A sales agent confirms verbal customer agreement","then":"The quote moves to the new status, and the transition is logged with the agent identity and timestamp"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Reporting or dashboards filter by status = 'confirmed' across entity types","expected_behavior":"If the same term is reused, reports would conflate paid orders with unpaid verbal agreements, producing incorrect revenue and fulfillment metrics."},{"scenario":"API consumers query 'confirmed' status without specifying entity type","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous results — some records have payment, some do not. Integration partners may assume payment occurred and trigger downstream fulfillment prematurely."},{"scenario":"A customer verbally agrees (quote 'confirmed') but never pays","expected_behavior":"If terminology is shared, support staff may misread the quote as a paid commitment and escalate incorrectly."},{"scenario":"A quote is 'confirmed' and later the customer requests changes","expected_behavior":"Unlike confirmed orders (immutable), the feature request does not specify immutability for quotes — creating a silent inconsistency in what 'confirmed' guarantees."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"HIGH RISK — Conflating paid (order confirmed) with unpaid (quote confirmed) statuses can lead to premature revenue recognition, incorrect financial reporting, and fulfillment of uncompensated work.","operational":"Developers, support staff, and operations teams will lose trust in the status field. Every query, filter, and report touching 'confirmed' must now disambiguate by entity type — a fragile and error-prone pattern.","user":"Sales agents, operations staff, and customers viewing order/quote status will encounter the same word meaning different things depending on context, increasing errors and support burden.","risk":"Domain language collision is a category of bug that is invisible in unit tests but causes systemic misunderstandings. It is extremely difficult to fix after adoption because it requires retraining humans and migrating data.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Sales Agents","impact":"May confuse quote confirmation (verbal) with order confirmation (paid) when communicating with customers or operations."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Revenue reports filtering on 'confirmed' may incorrectly include unpaid verbal agreements."},{"group":"Operations / Fulfillment","impact":"Risk of beginning fulfillment on verbally-agreed quotes that have not been paid."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Every query, event handler, and integration touching 'confirmed' status must now branch on entity type — increased complexity and bug surface."},{"group":"API Integration Partners","impact":"External systems consuming status values will receive 'confirmed' with contradictory semantics depending on entity type."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction: 'confirmed' is explicitly defined as paid+immutable for orders, and the feature request explicitly defines 'confirmed' for quotes as unpaid+verbal. Both definitions are stated, and both cannot coexist without violating ubiquitous language integrity. Confidence is low because this cannot be implemented as specified without domain corruption.","high_confidence_areas":["The existing definition of 'confirmed' for orders is explicit and established","The proposed definition for quotes explicitly contradicts the existing one on payment state","The collision will cause real downstream harm in reporting, integrations, and human communication"],"low_confidence_areas":[],"assumptions":["The order and quote lifecycles share a UI, API, or reporting surface where term collision would be visible","No separate bounded context with an anti-corruption layer exists to isolate quote terminology from order terminology"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The term 'confirmed' has an established domain definition (paid + immutable + queued for fulfillment) on Orders. The feature request proposes reusing 'confirmed' on Quotes with a contradictory definition (verbal agreement + NOT paid + mutability unspecified). These two definitions are mutually exclusive within a shared ubiquitous language.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Agents","Finance","Operations","Engineering","API Partners"],"options":["A) Rename the quote status to 'accepted' or 'verbally_agreed' to preserve the existing meaning of 'confirmed'","B) Rename the order status away from 'confirmed' (high migration cost, breaks existing integrations)","C) Treat Quotes and Orders as separate bounded contexts with explicit anti-corruption layer (high complexity, still confuses humans)"],"recommendation":"Option A — rename the quote status. The order definition is established, encoded, and depended upon. The quote status is new and has zero adoption cost. Use 'accepted' for the quote verbal-agreement state."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Reusing the term 'confirmed' for quotes with a meaning that directly contradicts its established meaning on orders","business_rule_broken":"BR-4: Each term in the ubiquitous language must have exactly one meaning across shared contexts. 'Confirmed' would mean both 'paid and immutable' (orders) and 'not paid, verbal only' (quotes)."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics/courier system with an existing order lifecycle where 'confirmed' has a precise, established meaning (paid + queued for fulfillment + immutable). A new feature request proposes reusing the term 'confirmed' for quotes, but with a fundamentally different semantic meaning (verbal agreement, no payment, not immutable).","bounded_context":"Order Management / Quoting — the collision occurs at the boundary between the Quote lifecycle and the Order lif

content.thinking:
This is a domain analysis task where the term "confirmed" is being used in two different contexts with different meanings. Let me analyze this carefully.

The existing domain: "confirmed" order = paid + queued for fulfillment + immutable
New request: "confirmed" quote = verbal agreement + no payment yet

This is a clear contradiction/ambiguity in the ubiquitous language. The same term "confirmed" means fundamentally different things in two contexts. One implies payment completed, the other explicitly states payment has NOT occurred.

This is a contradiction - two explicit definitions of the same term that conflict.
